name: The Chow Kit - an Ormond Hotel
detail: Definitely, I would suggest to everyone this beautiful small Hotel, designed till small details to make you feel comfortable. The rooms are nice,friendly staff, great restaurant and the perfect location. I need to give my compliments to the barman Michelle for the cocktails, hospitality and suggestions regarding the places in Kuala Lumpur. On my next trip to KL, I will definitely choose the same hotel. Thanks Marija :)
area: Chowkit / Putra WTC,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: MoMo's Kuala Lumpur
detail: The hotel is a great place to relax in a hectic city. We stayed for 3 nights and wish we could have stayed longer! The room was very modern, the bed super comfortable with a great view over the city. The best bit was the shower though - the best shower we had in 3 weeks of travelling around Malaysia!! The location is v good, around 20 mins walk to the Petronas Towers and Independence Square (I think thats what it is called). There are lots of restaurants nearby including a great halal Chinese opposite - the noodles are amazing, just like we had in China. The food at the hotel is also really good, I haven't had avocado in 4 months - loved it! We would definitely stay here again.
area: Chowkit / Putra WTC,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: Deface Platinum 2 Kuala Lumpur
detail: Hotel pool has the most amazing views day and night…
area: Kuala Lumpur City Centre,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: Tune Hotel KLIA Aeropolis (Airport Hotel)
detail: the staff were helpful, the room has enough amenities which we needed for a night of stay, even though the size of the room is pretty small, it isnt a big problem if you just stay there for a night or two, surely you wouldn't pick the hotel for a week of stay and for the travels in Kuala Lumpur city, the bus service to another Tune Hotel (which is closer to the airport) is right at hand, but needed to be booked in advance when you check in so don't forget to do that if you need it
area: Sepang / Kuala Lumpur International Airport,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: Tune Hotel KLIA-KLIA2, Airport Transit Hotel
detail: Always stay here if I need a stopover hotel in Kuala Lumpur. The service is friendly and personable, the room smallish but with a very comfortable bed and a great clean modern compact bathroom. TV reception and wifi reception both good. Drinks/snacks in the lobby very reasonably priced and love the inner courtyard with it's lounges and umbrellas for a drink and to relax. Had breakfast here for the first time and it's a good spread with variety and choice. Would be even better if proper coffee would be included😀 So close to the airport and it's shops and if you have some spare time a free shuttle bus leaves from in front of exit airport to the DFS store complex. tip: try the Indian food at the NZ curry house diagonally from the hotel (at airport) . Will most definitely use TUNE hotel KLIA2 again when next in KL
area: Sepang / Kuala Lumpur International Airport,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: The Bed KLCC
detail: I had a 13 hour layover in Kuala Lumpur on my way to Kathmandu, so I decided to go out the airport and see Petronas Twin Towers and have a decent place to sleep for the night as I arrived around 10 pm from Hongkong. I actually booked at The Bed in advance and I just paid around 800PHP via Agoda for one night! I took the female solo pod which consists of a shoe locker outside, a dental kit, towel, a locker beneath the bed and a bed with cover. That is already great because the accommodation is quite good, cozy, and good for solo traveller like me. LOCATION: It took me around 45 minutes to 1 hour to get to The Bed from the airport giving that the KLIA Expres travel time to KL Sentral is 28 to 30 minutes. From KL Sentral, get a (coin) ticket via RapidKL to Dang Wangi station (if you don't mind walking around 10 minutes to hotel),turn left,take the overpass,walk straight until you saw Bukit Nanas station. Cross the street. The nearest station from the hotel is Bukit Nanas station,which I took from the hotel to KL Sentral that cost me 3.70 MYR. I don't saw in the map that Bukit Nanas station will be passed by using Gombak line from KL Sentral because it is in the Titiwangsa line which you can also take,just walk from KL Sentral to Mid Valley station. The Bed is in the Vortex building, beside 7-11 so you will be able to see it clearly. RECEPTION: I checked in near midnight, and the receptionist is a Filipino and also some other Filipinos are checking in that time. The check in and check out was smooth and incredibly fast. INTERNET CONNECTION: Wi-Fi is reliable and good. I can still do some video calls while at the washroom and any part of the hotel. FOOD: The food was not that good, without variety. Breakfast was only a Malaysian rice (with peanuts,dried fish and half hard boiled egg) and breads. The drinks was water, coffee and tea. I hope they will add more in the future. Other than that, I can say that I can re

detail: The room is clean and comfortable and near with NU Sentral.

name: The RuMa Hotel and Residences
detail: 1 of the best hotels i have stayed in , undoubtedly. Will come back again and again for sure. Thanks Ruma. ❤️
area: Kuala Lumpur City Centre,Kuala Lumpur - View on map

name: One World Hotel
detail: This is the third time we used One World Hotel and it is our preferred hotel in Kuala Lumpur city. It is ideally located within lots of varieties of shops, supermarkets, departmental stores, restaurants and variety food stalls. The hotel location removes the need to travel elsewhere for meals and shoppings unless for sight seeing. The hotel facilities are excellant, room is large, clean & comfortable, breakfast good with great variety of choices and hotel staffs all round are friendly and with a 'Smile'.
area: One Utama / Damansara,Kuala Lumpur - View on map
